variant: give me the complete guide: table of products, weekly timeline, checklist, and key stats for starting skincare from zero

request.message: give me the complete guide: table of products, weekly timeline, checklist, and key stats for starting skincare from zero

response_body.response:
# skincare from zero: complete starter guide your docs have the system design principles but not a full product list or week-by-week protocol. I'll build this using your profile (21m, normal weight, 7am wake) and the barrier-first framework from your evidence, then fill in standard starter products. **core principle:** barrier first, one active at a time, SPF non-negotiable.

no overload. ---     --- ## key notes **barrier repair is the foundation.** ceramides + panthenol in your PM moisturizer. if you skip this and jump to actives, you'll damage the barrier and make acne worse. **one active at a time.** azelaic is gentler than retinoids and works well for acne + redness.

start there if you have acne; if your skin is clear, you can skip straight to a low-dose retinoid (adapalene 0.1%). **SPF is non-negotiable.** UV worsens pigmentation, breaks down collagen, and increases inflammation. apply every AM, even on cloudy days. **if irritation appears:** pause actives immediately, use only cleanser + moisturizer + SPF for 3–5 days, then restart at half frequency.

your wake time is 7am, so apply SPF right after your AM routine before leaving. PM routine before bed at 11pm gives your barrier 8 hours to repair.

products.name: La Roche-Posay Anthelios Melt-in Milk SPF 50+
products.description: High-protection broad-spectrum SPF 50 with cosmetic-elegant feel.

products.name: CeraVe Moisturizing Cream (Tub)
products.description: Heavy occlusive cream — good for very dry / eczema-prone skin.

products.name: CeraVe Foaming Facial Cleanser
products.description: Foaming yet non-stripping — for oily/acne-prone skin types.

products.name: The Ordinary Azelaic Acid Suspension 10%
products.description: Azelaic acid for redness, PIH, and mild rosacea — pregnancy-safe.

products.name: CeraVe PM Facial Moisturizing Lotion
products.description: Lightweight ceramide + niacinamide moisturizer for nightly use.

rows: azelaic 10-20% (if acne-prone) OR skip if clear
one active only; azelaic is gentler than retinoid to start
SPF 30+ (EltaMD UV clear or La Roche-Posay anthelios)

steps.label: week 1–2: establish baseline
steps.detail: cleanse AM + PM. moisturize + SPF AM.  moisturize PM. no actives yet.  watch for irritation, dryness, or sensitivity.

steps.label: week 3–4: introduce one active
steps.detail: if skin is clear: start azelaic 10% PM, 3x/week (mon/wed/fri). if acne-prone: azelaic PM daily after week 4.  if clear + no irritation: skip azelaic, move to retinoid week 5.

steps.label: week 5–8: stabilize active
steps.detail: azelaic or retinoid at chosen frequency. barrier stays strong (ceramides + panthenol every PM).  no second active yet. assess: less acne, less redness, skin feels hydrated.

steps.label: week 9+: optimize or add second active
steps.detail: if barrier is solid and skin tolerates first active: consider low-dose retinoid (adapalene 0.1%) PM on alternate nights.  keep azelaic or drop it. SPF every AM, no exceptions.

items: buy: gentle cleanser, ceramide + panthenol moisturizer, SPF 30+.  optional: azelaic 10% or adapalene 0.1%.
week 1: cleanse AM + PM, moisturize + SPF AM, moisturize PM only. no actives.
week 3: if skin is calm, add azelaic 10% PM 3x/week (mon/wed/fri).  if irritation appears, pause and extend week 1–2 another 7 days.
week 5+: increase azelaic to daily PM if tolerated, OR switch to retinoid PM 2–3x/week. never both at once.
every AM: cleanse, moisturize, SPF.  non-negotiable.
every PM: cleanse, active (if introduced), moisturize with ceramides + panthenol.
if redness, stinging, or dryness appears: pause actives, use only cleanser + moisturizer + SPF for 3–5 days.

cards.label: baseline phase (no actives)
cards.hint: let barrier stabilize before introducing treatment

cards.label: rule: never stack
cards.hint: acids + retinoids + vitamin c together = barrier damage

cards.label: every single AM
cards.hint: UV worsens acne, pigmentation, and collagen breakdown

cards.label: time to first visible change
cards.hint: acne reduction or texture improvement with consistent routine
